hypothesis: Incorporating three-dimensional conformational information—such as atomic coordinates or distance matrices derived from low-energy conformers—as an additional eighth modality within the STR-Bamba unified vocabulary will further improve cross-representational alignment in the latent space and yield statistically significant performance gains on quantum mechanical property prediction tasks (QM9, QM8) compared to the current seven-modality model, because 3D geometry encodes electronic structure information orthogonal to all existing string-based representations and will occupy a distinct yet bridging cluster in the latent space that reduces the prediction error specifically for properties sensitive to molecular conformation such as dipole moment and electronic gap.
